1486	read `Θ_src` and codomain read `Θ_tgt`, this transpose is the extension of scalars along
1487	`ψ : R → R'` of the `Spec R`-section reading `ρ` of `inner` (Seam 2); on the generator `r' ⊗ m`
1488	it returns `(1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`, exactly the inverse of the regrouping isomorphism
1489	`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`. See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. -/
1490	theorem base_change_mate_gstar_transpose {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1491	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1492	    (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1493	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1494	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).map
1495	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
1496	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
1497	                    (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).unit.app (tilde M)) ≫
1498	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp
1499	                  (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)) (Spec.map φ)).hom.app _ ≫
1500	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
1501	                  (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w).hom.app _ ≫
1502	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp
1503	                  (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)) (Spec.map ψ)).inv.app _) ≫
1504	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map ψ)).counit.app
1505	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
1506	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
1507	                (tilde M)))) ≫
1508	      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
1509	      = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv := by
1510	  -- PARTIAL: split `Γ(g^*(inner) ≫ ε_g) = Γ(g^*(inner)) ≫ Γ(ε_g)` by functoriality of
1511	  -- `moduleSpecΓFunctor`. After this the goal is
1512	  --   `Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ (Γ(g^*(inner)) ≫ Γ(ε_g)) ≫ Θ_tgt = regroupEquiv.inv`.
1513	  rw [Functor.map_comp]
1514	  -- REMAINING (the genuine crux): the pullback-dictionary coherence. Conjugating
1515	  -- `Γ(g^*(inner)) ≫ Γ(ε_g)` by `Θ_src = base_change_mate_domain_read` and
1516	  -- `Θ_tgt = base_change_mate_codomain_read` replaces `g^* = (Spec ψ)^*` of a tilde by extension
1517	  -- of scalars along `ψ` (Lemma `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`), turning it into
1518	  -- `extendScalars ψ` (≡ `LinearMap.lTensor R'`) applied to the `Spec R`-section reading `ρ` of
1519	  -- `inner` supplied by Seam 2 (`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`, RHS = `base_change_mate_inner_value`).
1520	  -- Since `ρ : m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`, its `R'`-base change sends `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`, which is
1521	  -- `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv.inv` on generators (both `R'`-linear, `ext` on `1 ⊗ m`). The
1522	  -- coherence is the `pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ` / counit naturality identification of the conjugated
1523	  -- `g^*`-leg with `extendScalars ψ ∘ ρ`; it is the Mathlib-absent mate-unwinding over the generic
1524	  -- pullback square. Uses: `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex` (Seam 2), `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`.
1525	  sorry
1526	
1527	/-- **The section-level base-change map is the base change of the unit.** With the domain and
1528	codomain pinned by `base_change_mate_domain_read` (`Θ_src`) and `base_change_mate_codomain_read`
1529	(`Θ_tgt`), the conjugated section-level base-change map
1530	`Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt : R' ⊗_R M ⟶ (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` is the `R'`-base change of the algebraic
1531	unit `η_M : M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`, `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`: on the generator `r' ⊗ m` it returns
1532	`r' • ((1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m) = (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`. Equivalently it equals the *inverse* of the regrouping
1533	isomorphism `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`, i.e.
1534	`Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv`. See blueprint
1535	`lem:base_change_mate_section_identity`.
1536	
1537	The argument is carried out directly on the module of global sections; no abstract adjoint-mate
1538	identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By `ModuleCat` hom-extensionality it suffices to
1539	check on the `R'`-module generators `1 ⊗ₜ x` (`ext x`), leaving the concrete per-generator identity
1540	`(Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt) (1 ⊗ x) = regroupEquiv.inv (1 ⊗ x)`. By construction `Γ(θ)` is, read on
1541	global sections through the two tilde dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` /
1542	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso` packaged in `Θ_src`, `Θ_tgt`, the extension of scalars along `ψ : R → R'`
1543	of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars along `A → A ⊗_R R'` (`m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); evaluating
1544	through the `R'`-action on the target gives `1 ⊗ x ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ x`, which is exactly
1545	`regroupEquiv.inv` on generators (both sides `R'`-linear). The remaining obligation is the
1546	identification of `Γ(pushforwardBaseChangeMap …)` on the generator with the unit value, i.e. the
1547	mate-unwinding coherence over the generic pullback square; it is Mathlib-absent (typed `sorry` at the
1548	per-generator node below). With it discharged, `base_change_mate_generator_trace` is a one-line
1549	corollary. -/
1550	theorem base_change_mate_section_identity {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1551	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1552	    (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1553	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1554	        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1555	          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1556	          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1557	          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
1558	      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
1559	      = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv := by
1560	  -- COUNIT FACTORIZATION (blueprint Seam 3 step): `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is by definition the
1561	  -- `(g^* ⊣ g_*)`-adjunction transpose `((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm inner`
1562	  -- of the inner pushforward comparison `inner`. By `Adjunction.homEquiv_counit` this transpose is
1563	  -- `g^*(inner) ≫ counit`, with no opaque adjunction transpose remaining. After this rewrite the goal
1564	  -- is exactly the statement of Seam 3 (`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`).
1565	  unfold pushforwardBaseChangeMap
1566	  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_counit]
1567	  exact base_change_mate_gstar_transpose ψ φ M
1568	
1569	/-- **Generator trace of the section-level base-change map.** With the domain and codomain pinned
1570	by `base_change_mate_domain_read` (`Θ_src`) and `base_change_mate_codomain_read` (`Θ_tgt`), the
1571	conjugated section-level base-change map `Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(α) ≫ Θ_tgt` — a map `R' ⊗_R M ⟶
1572	(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` — is an isomorphism. The generator trace of the blueprint shows it sends
1573	`r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`, i.e. it is `cancelBaseChange⁻¹` (after the `A ⊗_R R' ≅ R' ⊗_R A`
1574	regrouping); since `cancelBaseChange` is a Mathlib `LinearEquiv` with no flatness hypothesis, the
1575	conjugate is an isomorphism. This `IsIso` form is the iso-consequence the affine close consumes
1576	(mirroring the parent `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`); the proof obligation is the
1577	adjoint-mate unwinding of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` on global sections through the two proved
1578	tilde dictionaries. See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace`. -/
1579	theorem base_change_mate_generator_trace {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1580	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1581	    IsIso ((base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1582	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1583	        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1584	          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1585	          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1586	          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
1587	      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom) := by
1588	  -- By the section identity `base_change_mate_section_identity` the conjugated map equals
1589	  -- `(base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv`, the inverse of a `ModuleCat` isomorphism, hence an
1590	  -- isomorphism.
1591	  rw [base_change_mate_section_identity]
1592	  infer_instance
1593	
1594	/-! ## Section-level value of the affine base-change map -/
1595	
1596	/-- **Section-level value of the affine base-change map.** In the affine–affine model — base ring
1597	maps `ψ : R ⟶ R'` (the base change `g = Spec ψ`) and `φ : R ⟶ A` (the affine morphism
1598	`f = Spec φ`), with `F = M^~` for an `A`-module `M`, the canonical pullback square supplying
1599	`g' = pullback.fst`, `f' = pullback.snd` — the global-sections incarnation `Γ(α)` of the
1600	base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism of `R'`-modules.
1601	
1602	By the four affine dictionaries the domain `Γ(g^*(f_* M^~))` reads as `R' ⊗_R M` and the codomain
1603	`Γ(f'_*(g')^* M^~)` reads as `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M`, under which `Γ(α)` is the canonical cancellation
1604	isomorphism `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (in the orientation
1605	`Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no
1606	flatness hypothesis, `Γ(α)` is an isomorphism. This is the section-level content of the affine
1607	close: it is the per-affine-chart input to `base_change_map_affine_local` once the locality
1608	reduction has restricted an arbitrary square to this affine–affine model. See blueprint
1609	`lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`.
1610	
1611	The proof is the 4-step generator trace of the blueprint: unwind the adjoint mate of the
1612	`((g')^*, (g')_*)`-unit on global sections through `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and
1613	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`, landing on `cancelBaseChange⁻¹`. The mate-unwinding coherence over the
1614	generic pullback square `pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` is the genuine crux and is the
1615	outstanding obligation (typed `sorry` below). -/
1616	theorem pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange
1617	    {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1618	    IsIso ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1619	      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1620	        (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1621	        (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1622	        (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M))) := by
1623	  -- Assemble the chain (blueprint `lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`): the domain
1624	  -- read `Θ_src` (`base_change_mate_domain_read`) and codomain read `Θ_tgt`
1625	  -- (`base_change_mate_codomain_read`, resting on the `pullbackSpecIso` leg identification
1626	  -- `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`) identify `Γ(α)`'s domain/codomain with `R' ⊗_R M` and
1627	  -- `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`; the generator trace `base_change_mate_generator_trace` shows the conjugate
1628	  -- `Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(α) ≫ Θ_tgt` is an isomorphism (it is `cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, no flatness).
1629	  -- Conjugating back, `Γ(α)` is itself an isomorphism.
1630	  haveI hconj := base_change_mate_generator_trace ψ φ M
1631	  set D := base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M with hD
1632	  set C := base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M with hC
1633	  set Γα := (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1634	    (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1635	      (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1636	      (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1637	      (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) with hΓα
1638	  have heq : Γα = D.hom ≫ (D.inv ≫ Γα ≫ C.hom) ≫ C.inv := by
1639	    simp [Category.assoc]
1640	  rw [heq]
1641	  infer_instance
1642	
1643	/-! ## The affine base-change lemma and its locality reduction -/
1644	
1645	/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
1646	and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
1647	as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
1648	the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
1649	affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
1650	the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
1651	by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
1652	carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
1653	`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
1654	See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
1655	theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
1656	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
1657	    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
1658	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
1659	  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H
1660	
1661	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
1662	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
1663	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
1664	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
1665	
1666	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
1667	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
1668	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
1669	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
1670	  -- FIRST REDUCTION (locality on `S'`): by `base_change_map_affine_local` it suffices to check
1671	  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.
1672	  -- This is exactly the locality reduction; the per-affine-open hypothesis is the affine–affine
1673	  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.
1674	  apply base_change_map_affine_local f g f' g' h F
1675	  intro U
1676	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.
1677	  --
1678	  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
1679	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
1680	  --     (above): in the affine–affine model `Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, hence an iso (no
1681	  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
1682	  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
1683	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
1684	  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
1685	  --
1686	  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
1687	  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
1688	  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ⊆ S'` and a chosen affine
1689	  -- `Spec R ⊆ S` containing `g(U)` — over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
1690	  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` — and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
1691	  -- …).app U` with the affine–affine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:
1692	  -- naturality of the adjunction transpose + pushforward-commutes-with-restriction). Then the
1693	  -- per-`U` goal is exactly `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (composed with the
1694	  -- tilde–Γ counit isos to pass `IsIso Γ(α) ⟹ IsIso α` on the QC chart). This restriction-
1695	  -- compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is itself Mathlib-absent and is the remaining
1696	  -- multi-hundred-LOC build for the unconditional general theorem.
1697	  -- See `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
1698	  sorry
1699	
1700	/-- **Flat base change, `i = 0` case.** If `g` is flat and `f` is quasi-compact
1701	and quasi-separated, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an
1702	isomorphism. Equivalently, in the affine situation `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
1703	with `A → B` flat, the comparison map `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B → H⁰(X_B, F_B)` is an
1704	isomorphism.
1705	
1706	Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH ("Flat base change"), the `i = 0` case. -/
1707	theorem flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g]
1708	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
1709	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
1710	  -- Proof strategy (Stacks 02KH, `i = 0`), deferred to a later iteration:
1711	  -- the statement is local on `S'`, so reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
1712	  -- with `A → B` flat.  Choose a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`.  Since `f`
1713	  -- is quasi-compact and quasi-separated the Čech complex of `𝒰` computes
1714	  -- `H⁰(X, F)`, and base change identifies `Čech(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Čech(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`
1715	  -- term by term via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`.  Flatness of `A → B`
1716	  -- makes `- ⊗_A B` exact, so it commutes with `H⁰`, giving the isomorphism
1717	  -- `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)`.  Needs the (missing) Čech-cohomology /
1718	  -- affine-cover infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`; see
1719	  -- `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
1720	  sorry
1721	
1722	end AlgebraicGeometry

1917	  \]
1918	  with \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
1919	  \(\eta^{g'}\) the unit of the \((g')\)-adjunction, and
1920	  \(w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) the commutativity of the square.
1921	
1922	  \emph{Why a direct substitution fails.} The identity cannot be obtained by substituting the
1923	  leg factorizations directly, because the legs \(g'\) and \(f'\) appear in multiple positions
1924	  simultaneously: as the index of the adjunction \(\eta^{g'}\), inside the equality proof
1925	  \(w\) parametrizing \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w\), inside the argument
1926	  \(f'_{*}\bigl((g')^{*}\widetilde M\bigr)\) of the codomain comparison
1927	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain
1928	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
1929	  The reindex is therefore restructured into three sub-steps so that the legs become explicit
1930	  free variables; it is \emph{not} a single rewrite.
1931	
1932	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
1933	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
1934	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
1935	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
1936	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
1937	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
1938	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
1939	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
1940	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
1941	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
1942	
1943	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
1944	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
1945	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
1946	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
1947	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
1948	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
1949	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
1950	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
1951	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
1952	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
1953	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
1954	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
1955	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
1956	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
1957	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
1958	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
1959	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
1960	  sections.
1961	
1962	  \emph{(iii) Reduction to Seam~1 via the leg-reindex engine.} With the legs substituted
1963	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
1964	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
1965	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), rewrites the unit factor
1966	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the
1967	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})\) coherence, into the affine
1968	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the
1969	  \(e\)-unit. Since \(e\) is an isomorphism of schemes, its pullback functor is an equivalence
1970	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an
1971	  isomorphism absorbed into the codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). The surviving
1972	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit has section value over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) equal to
1973	  the algebraic unit \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Seam~1
1974	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Composing the surviving factor with the
1975	  codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
1976	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) and reading over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
1977	  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) identifies all four factors with the restriction of
1978	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely
1979	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\)
1980	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}) — the
1981	  ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is
1982	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
1983	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.
1984	\end{proof}

2049	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
2050	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
2051	        lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso,
2052	        lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
2053	  This crux reads the \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose of the inner composite
2054	  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) over \(\operatorname{Spec} R'\): it is the live remaining obligation
2055	  of the affine section-level computation, and the section identity
2056	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity} follows from it directly. The transpose is
2057	  the extension of scalars along \(\psi\) of the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading
2058	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\), whose value is the
2059	  affine unit value of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}.
2060	
2061	  \emph{Counit factorization.} The base-change map
2062	  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is the adjunction transpose of
2063	  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) under \((g^* \dashv g_*)\) with \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\). By the
2064	  counit form of the hom-set adjunction (\(\operatorname{homEquiv}\)-counit identity for the
2065	  transpose), it factors as \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the counit
2066	  \(\varepsilon_g\), with no opaque transpose remaining:
2067	  \(\Gamma(\theta) = \Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\).
2068	
2069	  \emph{The concrete counit coherence — why it is not a bare computation.} Conjugating
2070	  \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by \(\varepsilon_g\) by the domain identification
2071	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and the codomain
2072	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read})
2073	  requires the pullback dictionary \(\Pi_{\psi}\) of
2074	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} — which reads \(g^* = (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)^*\) of a
2075	  tilde as extension of scalars along \(\psi\) — to interact with the geometric counit
2076	  \(\varepsilon_g\). This interaction is \emph{not} a definitional reduction: \(\Pi_{\psi}\) is
2077	  built abstractly as the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints comparison, evaluated from the
2078	  natural-isomorphism of right adjoints \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso})
2079	  via the conjugate-of-isomorphisms equivalence; concretely \(\Pi_{\psi}\) is the component at the
2080	  module of
2081	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{conjIso}(\mathrm{adj}_L, \mathrm{adj}_R)^{-1}(\gamma_{\psi})\bigr)^{-1}\),
2082	  where \(\mathrm{adj}_L = (\widetilde{(-)}_R \dashv \Gamma_R) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\) is the
2083	  composite ``tilde then pullback'' adjunction and
2084	  \(\mathrm{adj}_R = (\,{}^{\psi}\!(-) \dashv {}_{\psi}(-)) . (\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv
2085	  \Gamma_{R'})\) is the composite ``extension of scalars then tilde'' adjunction. Because the
2086	  dictionary is opaque in this way, the counit cannot be simplified against it by reflexivity
2087	  or by definitional unfolding; the identification must be made through the abstract conjugate calculus.
2088	
2089	  \emph{Conjugation via the composed-adjunction counit-triangle identity.} The required identity

2089	  \emph{Conjugation via the composed-adjunction counit-triangle identity.} The required identity
2090	  is the counit-triangle (zig-zag) coherence for the \emph{composed} adjunction
2091	  \((\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv \Gamma_{R'}) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\), the same composite adjunction
2092	  whose left adjoint \(\Pi_{\psi}\) compares. Read concretely, this coherence states that the
2093	  dictionary \(\Pi_{\psi}\), composed with the geometric counit \(\varepsilon_g\) and the
2094	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit, equals the algebraic counit of the
2095	  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction \(({}^{\psi}\!(-) \dashv {}_{\psi}(-))\) along
2096	  \(\psi\); equivalently, conjugating \(\Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\) by the tilde dictionaries
2097	  identifies it with the module-level adjunction counit of
2098	  the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\). This is the dual companion of the
2099	  unit-across-conjugate coherence (Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}) used in Seam~1.
2100	  Applying it, conjugation replaces the transpose by extension of scalars along \(\psi\)
2101	  applied to the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading \(\rho\) of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) —
2102	  the canonical \(R\)-linear map \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), the affine unit
2103	  value of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}: the geometric counit collapses to
2104	  the module-level counit, and the residual \(g^*\)-leg becomes \({}^{\psi}\!(-) = \) extension of
2105	  scalars along \(\psi\) applied to \(\rho\).
2106	
2107	  \emph{Identification on generators.} Since \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), its
2108	  extension of scalars along \(\psi\) sends
2109	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). This
2110	  is precisely the inverse of the regrouping isomorphism
2111	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on generators, and both maps are
2112	  \(R'\)-linear, so they coincide.
2113	\end{proof}

1217	            (T := (R' : Type u))).hom ≫
1218	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
1219	            (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u)))))

1221	            (T := (R' : Type u))).hom ≫

1223	            (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom)),

1227	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
1228	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))

1230	        ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
1231	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
1232	            (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M) := by

1234	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
1235	  set e := pullbackSpecIso (R := (R : Type u)) (S := (A : Type u)) (T := (R' : Type u)) with he

1260	/-! ### Seam 2, step (iii): the mate-unwinding crux, cut into five atomic links
1261	
1262	The five lemmas `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_{unitExpand, gammaDistribute, eCancel,
1263	affineUnit, innerMatch}` decompose the step-(iii) obligation of
1264	`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs` into one mathematical move each. See blueprint
1265	`lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_{unitExpand,…}`. -/
1266	
1267	/-- **(iii-1) Unit expansion: inverting the comp-coherence.** For composable `a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`,
1268	`b : X₂ ⟶ X₃`, a module `N` on `X₃`, and `g' = a ≫ b`, the bare `(g')`-unit expands as the
1269	four-factor composite `η^b ≫ b_*(η^a) ≫ pushforwardComp(a,b).hom ≫ g'_*(pullbackComp(a,b).hom)`.
1270	This is the inversion of the leg-reindex engine `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp`: post-composing its
1271	identity with `g'_*(pullbackComp.hom)` collapses the trailing `pullbackComp.inv` (functoriality of
1272	`g'_*` on `inv ≫ hom = id`). See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`. -/
1273	theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
1274	    (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules) :
1275	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N
1276	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1277	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1278	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1279	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
1280	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
1281	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫
1282	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1283	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N) := by
1284	  rw [pullbackPushforward_unit_comp a b N]
1285	  -- The two trailing `(a ≫ b)_*`-images cancel (`pullbackComp.inv ≫ pullbackComp.hom = 𝟙`), but a
1286	  -- spurious `X.Modules` instance diamond under single-file elaboration makes `rw [Category.assoc]`
1287	  -- / `rw [hI]` / `simp only [Iso.inv_hom_id_app]` misfire on the goal's locked composition. We
1288	  -- prove the cancellation on FRESH terms (where the rewrites match) and discharge the goal in
1289	  -- term mode (`exact`), which checks defeq and tolerates the diamond.
1290	  have hI : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app N) ≫
1291	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N)
1292	      = 𝟙 _ := by
1293	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
1294	  have hfin := (congrArg (fun z =>
1295	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N ≫ z) hI).trans
1296	    (Category.comp_id _)
1297	  exact ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans hfin).symm
1298	
1299	/-- **(iii-2) Distribute the expansion through a functor (e.g. `(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ`).** Applying any
1300	functor `F` out of `X₃.Modules` to the `unitExpand` four-factor expansion of the `(a ≫ b)`-unit
1301	distributes over the four factors, by functoriality (`F (u ≫ v) = F u ≫ F v`). In the assembly `F`
1302	is `(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R`, giving the four `Γ`-image factors of the blueprint. See blueprint
1303	`lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute`. -/
1304	theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
1305	    {D : Type*} [Category D] (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules)
1306	    (F : X₃.Modules ⥤ D) :
1307	    F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N)
1308	      = (F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N) ≫
1309	          F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1310	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1311	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫
1312	          F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
1313	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)))) ≫
1314	        F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1315	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N)) := by
1316	  -- After `unitExpand` the goal is pure functoriality (`F (u ≫ v) = F u ≫ F v`), but the
1317	  -- `X.Modules` instance diamond blocks `rw/simp [Functor.map_comp]`; we apply `F.map_comp` in term
1318	  -- mode (elaboration unifies the instances up to defeq).
1319	  rw [base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand a b N]
1320	  exact (F.map_comp _ _).trans (congrArg (· ≫ F.map _)
1321	    ((F.map_comp _ _).trans (congrArg (F.map _ ≫ ·) (F.map_comp _ _))))
1322

257	variable [Category.{v₁} C] [Category.{v₂} D]
258	variable {L₁ L₂ : C ⥤ D} {R₁ R₂ : D ⥤ C}

261	/-- Given two adjunctions `L₁ ⊣ R₁` and `L₂ ⊣ R₂` both between categories `C`, `D`, there is a
262	bijection between natural transformations `L₂ ⟶ L₁` and natural transformations `R₁ ⟶ R₂`. This is
263	defined as a special case of `mateEquiv`, where the two "vertical" functors are identity, modulo
264	composition with the unitors. Corresponding natural transformations are called `conjugateEquiv`.
265	TODO: Generalise to when the two vertical functors are equivalences rather than being exactly `𝟭`.
266	
267	Furthermore, this bijection preserves (and reflects) isomorphisms, i.e. a transformation is an iso
268	iff its image under the bijection is an iso, see e.g. `CategoryTheory.conjugateIsoEquiv`.
269	This is in contrast to the general case `mateEquiv` which does not in general have this property.
270	-/
271	@[simps!]
272	def conjugateEquiv : (L₂ ⟶ L₁) ≃ (R₁ ⟶ R₂) :=
273	  calc
274	    (L₂ ⟶ L₁) ≃ (𝟭 C ⋙ L₂ ⟶ L₁ ⋙ 𝟭 D) := (Iso.homCongr L₂.leftUnitor L₁.rightUnitor).symm
275	    _ ≃ TwoSquare _ _ _ _ := (TwoSquare.equivNatTrans _ _ _ _).symm
276	    _ ≃ _ := mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂
277	    _ ≃ (R₁ ⋙ 𝟭 C ⟶ 𝟭 D ⋙ R₂) := TwoSquare.equivNatTrans _ _ _ _
278	    _ ≃ (R₁ ⟶ R₂) := R₁.rightUnitor.homCongr R₂.leftUnitor

281	theorem conjugateEquiv_counit (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (d : D) :
282	    L₂.map ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
283	      α.app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
284	  simp

287	theorem conjugateEquiv_counit_symm (α : R₁ ⟶ R₂) (d : D) :
288	    L₂.map (α.app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
289	      ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
290	    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
291	    exact (conjugateEquiv_counit adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) d)

294	theorem unit_conjugateEquiv (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (c : C) :
295	    adj₁.unit.app _ ≫ (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _ =
296	      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) := by
297	  dsimp [conjugateEquiv]
298	  rw [id_comp, comp_id]
299	  have := unit_mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ (L₂.leftUnitor.hom ≫ α ≫ L₁.rightUnitor.inv) c
300	  dsimp at this
301	  rw [this]
302	  simp

307	      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _) := by
308	    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
309	    exact (unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) c)

332	variable {C : Type u₁} {D : Type u₂}
333	variable [Category.{v₁} C] [Category.{v₂} D]
334	variable {L₁ L₂ L₃ : C ⥤ D} {R₁ R₂ R₃ : D ⥤ C}
335	variable (adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁) (adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂) (adj₃ : L₃ ⊣ R₃)

337	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
338	theorem conjugateEquiv_comp (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (β : L₃ ⟶ L₂) :
339	    conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α ≫ conjugateEquiv adj₂ adj₃ β =
340	      conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₃ (β ≫ α) := by
341	  ext d
342	  dsimp [conjugateEquiv, mateEquiv]
343	  have vcomp := mateEquiv_vcomp adj₁ adj₂ adj₃
344	    (L₂.leftUnitor.hom ≫ α ≫ L₁.rightUnitor.inv)
345	    (L₃.leftUnitor.hom ≫ β ≫ L₂.rightUnitor.inv)
346	  have vcompd := congr_app vcomp d

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean

Lane: FBC — prove the Seam-3 crux `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (decl now @1490 after
edits; sorry now @~1547). Module builds GREEN (`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange`
→ "Build completed successfully (8318 jobs)"). Sorry landscape unchanged in COUNT (gstar @1490,
dead-code `fstar_reindex_legs`, `affineBaseChange` @1693, `flatBaseChange` @1733); the gstar sorry now
carries genuine partial progress + the precise route.

## base_change_mate_gstar_transpose (decl @1490, sorry @~1547)

### Attempt 1 — counit split + iso-reframing + route identification
- **Approach:** Establish the structural setup the proof needs and isolate the residual coherence.
  1. `rw [Functor.map_comp]` (was already present) splits
     `Γ_{R'}(g^*(θ_in) ≫ ε_g) = Γ_{R'}(g^*(θ_in)) ≫ Γ_{R'}(ε_g)`.
  2. **NEW:** `rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv]` moves `Θ_src`, `regroupEquiv`, `Θ_tgt` to the
     RHS, isolating the two geometric Γ-factors on the LHS:
     `Γ_{R'}(g^*(θ_in)) ≫ Γ_{R'}(ε_g) = (Θ_src.hom ≫ regroupEquiv.inv) ≫ Θ_tgt.inv`.
     This is the exact shape the counit-conjugate calculus consumes. Verified compiling.
- **Result:** PARTIAL (structure in place; residual coherence isolated + route pinned). Not closed.
- **Key finding — the route (dual of the proven Seam 1):** gstar is the COUNIT companion of the
  proven `base_change_mate_unit_value` (Seam 1), which closed its *unit* coherence via
  `CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv_symm`. The dual Mathlib lemma is
  **`CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_counit_symm`** (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean:287`):
  `L₂.map (α.app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d = ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d`.
  Instantiate it — mirroring `pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ`'s own construction — at
    - `adjL = (tilde.adjunction R).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map ψ))`
    - `adjR = (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj ψ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction R')`
    - `α    = gammaPushforwardNatIso ψ`,
  so `(conjugateEquiv adjL adjR).symm α = pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ` (the ψ-dictionary baked into
  `Θ_src`/`Θ_tgt`). This rewrites the geometric counit `ε_g`, conjugated by the tilde dictionaries,
  into the algebraic extend/restrict-scalars counit along ψ.

### Why this seam is HARDER than Seam 1 (bundles two pieces)
After the counit-conjugate rewrite, two things remain to assemble — this is why the iter-020 route
swap promoted gstar to "the live crux that bundles everything":
  - **(a) inner reindex of `θ_in` over Spec R.** `Γ_R(θ_in) = ρ : m ↦ (1⊗1)⊗m`
    (`= base_change_mate_inner_value`). The existing Seam-2 lemma `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`
    states EXACTLY this but is **sorry-backed** (its `…_legs` apparatus @~1333 carries a dead `sorry`),
    so it CANNOT be cited — doing so makes gstar non-axiom-clean. This content must be **reproven
    inline**. The buried `(g')`-unit value inside `θ_in` is Seam 1 `base_change_mate_unit_value`
    (proved, axiom-clean) — reusable. The transparent pushforwardComp/Congr coherences collapse under
    Γ via the proved `gammaMap_pushforwardComp_{hom,inv}_eq_id` / `gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom`.
  - **(b) generator close.** `extendScalars ψ (ρ)` then the algebraic counit equals `regroupEquiv.inv`
    on `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m` (both R'-linear; one-generator `ext` against
    `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`, whose value `cancelBaseChange_tmul` is already unfolded there).

### Dead ends (do NOT repeat)
- **Per-generator brute force is a dead end.** `ext x` cleanly reduces to the goal on `1 ⊗ₜ[R] x`, but
  the LHS is then the FULL opaque geometric composite (`moduleSpecΓFunctor.map` of the geometric
  counit/pullback/pushforwardComp/unit) applied to `x`. Verified: neither `rfl` nor
  `simp [base_change_mate_domain_read, …codomain_read, …regroupEquiv]` evaluates it — the geometric
  counit/pullback/Γ have no element-level normal form. The abstract conjugate calculus is the only
  tractable route.
- **Routing through `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex` (Seam 2) is BANNED** — it is sorry-backed (dead
  `…_legs`); citing it forfeits axiom-cleanliness. Reprove its content inline (piece (a)).

### Concrete next-iter recipe
1. Reproduce Seam 1's proof skeleton (`base_change_mate_unit_value`, @987) but for the counit:
   `set adjL`, `set adjR`, `set β := gammaPushforwardNatIso ψ`; obtain
   `(conjugateEquiv adjL adjR).symm β.hom = pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ …` by `rfl` (Seam 1's `hpullinv`
   analog); feed `conjugateEquiv_counit_symm adjL adjR β.hom (·)` (Seam 1 used
   `unit_conjugateEquiv_symm`); use `Adjunction.comp_counit_app` to split adjL/adjR counits and the
   tilde right/left-triangle identities (`tilde.adjunction` (co)unit) for the Γ-fragment moves
   (analogs of Seam 1's `hClaimA`/`hgPTI`/`htri`).
2. Inline piece (a) via `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_{unitExpand,gammaDistribute}` (both
   PROVED, @1273/@1304) + `gammaMap_pushforwardComp_*` + Seam 1's value + `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp`
   (@1144, proved). This is the same content the dead `…_legs` tried to assemble — but only its
   step-(iii) `eCancel`/`innerMatch` telescoping carried the sorry; steps (i)/(ii) are sound.
3. Close piece (b) by `ext`/generator against `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`.

## Needs blueprint entry
None — no new declarations introduced this session (only a 2-`rw` reframing + comments inside the
existing `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`, which is already blueprinted as
`lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`). The route now uses `CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_counit_symm`
(Mathlib) — if a future planner wants it in `\uses{}`, it is the dual of `lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib`
already cited in the gstar blueprint proof's `\uses`.

## Outcome

**Target:** `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (the Seam-3 crux) in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`.

**Status:** Genuine partial progress committed; module builds **green** (`lake build … → Build completed successfully, 8318 jobs`). No new sorries, no axioms, no other declarations touched.
